explanations.2: This line checks if the PHP version is 8 or higher by using a shell math expression; the \>= means 'greater than or equal to' and the result (1 for true, 0 for false) determines which branch executes.
explanations.3: This printf command uses ANSI escape codes (the \e[1;...m parts) to print colored text to the terminal; specifically it makes the text bold and yellow.
explanations.5: Similar to line 3, this prints colored terminal text, but it's in the else branch so it only runs when PHP is version 7.
